title: CtrlVTON: Controllable Virtual Try-On via Visual-Instance-Prompt Segmentation
abstract: Virtual try-on (VTO) has made significant progress in realistically transferring garments onto a target person. Yet most systems give the user little control over how a garment should be worn -- its size (loose or fitted), style (e.g., tucked in or untucked, open or closed), and spatial placement on the body. We address this gap with two complementary contributions. First, we define and solve Visual-Instance-Prompt Segmentation via VIP-SAM: given a flatlay image of a garment, segment that specific instance in a photograph of a person wearing it. This is an instance-level task, distinct from the typically studied category-level segmentation. Second, we introduce CtrlVTON, a controllable VTO framework that recasts try-on as an image editing problem and adds segmentation masks as pixel-level control over garment layout, including style, size, and spatial placement on the body. VIP-SAM and CtrlVTON each achieve state-of-the-art results on their respective tasks. In particular, CtrlVTON generates images that follow user-provided layouts far more faithfully than the strongest proprietary editing systems while matching them on garment fidelity.
methods: The core method, VIP-SAM, enhances the segmentation of a specific instance of an object (e.g., a garment) in a query image by conditioning the query encoder on the support image features through cross-attention adapters at intermediate stages of the encoder. This approach ensures precise instance-level discrimination, even in the presence of occlusion, non-rigid deformation, and same-category distractors, making it suitable for e-commerce applications.
experiments: The experimental setup evaluates VIP-SAM on fashion segmentation tasks using the fashion segmentation benchmark and standard benchmarks COCO-20<sup>_i_</sup> and PASCAL-5<sup>_i_</sup>. VIP-SAM significantly outperforms existing methods, achieving state-of-the-art mIoU (FB-IoU) scores, particularly with the Hiera-L + DINOv3-H configuration, which achieves 97.2 (98.4) on the Fashion-val dataset. Qualitative results in Fig. 5 show VIP-SAM's superior performance in isolating queried instances, especially in complex scenarios with layered and similar-textured garments.
limitations: The paper identifies limitations in inpainting-based VTO, specifically noting that the generated output is highly sensitive to the size and shape of the mask, and is also influenced by the context outside the mask. These factors can lead to failure regions in the generated output, as illustrated in Fig. 11.
summary: The research paper introduces CtrlVTON, a method for controllable virtual try-on (VTO) that uses a novel segmentation technique called VIP-SAM. VIP-SAM improves the accuracy of segmenting specific items, like clothing, in images by leveraging cross-attention adapters to condition the query encoder on support image features. This method excels in handling occlusions and deformations, making it highly effective for e-commerce applications. However, the paper also notes that the VTO process can be sensitive to the size and shape of the segmentation mask and the surrounding context, which can sometimes lead to issues in the generated images.
